title: Gate on fast-tier p99 and standard-tier p95
preview: Summary: This PR adds tier-aware SLO gating to perf-canary for the Workload-aware Batching Defaults launch. We now fail the perf-canary run if: (1) FAST tier p99 latency regresses beyond threshold (tail-latency protection gate), or (2) STANDARD tier p95 latency regresses beyond threshold (steady-state gate). The int...
text: Summary: This PR adds tier-aware SLO gating to perf-canary for the Workload-aware Batching Defaults launch. We now fail the perf-canary run if: (1) FAST tier p99 latency regresses beyond threshold (tail-latency protection gate), or (2) STANDARD tier p95 latency regresses beyond threshold (steady-state gate). The intent is to stop merges that silently violate the published tier SLOs while still allowing legitimate improvements to land with a controlled baseline refresh process. Motivation: As we move batching config from ad-hoc per-route knobs to three customer-facing tiers (fast / standard / cost-efficient), we need CI guardrails that match what we’ll ship. Previously, canary gating was based on a single aggregate p95 and a single scenario per model. That missed fast-tier tail regressions (p99) and could overreact to noise in standard tier when concurrency ramps changed. What changed ...

title: add adaptive outlier suppression to canary evaluator
preview: Motivation: Canary evaluators occasionally trigger rollbacks due to short-lived outliers (e.g., transient downstream hiccups or probe skew) that do not reflect sustained regressions. This PR introduces an adaptive outlier suppression layer that reduces sensitivity to isolated spikes while preserving detection of rea...
text:
Motivation: Canary evaluators occasionally trigger rollbacks due to short-lived outliers (e.g., transient downstream hiccups or probe skew) that do not reflect sustained regressions. This PR introduces an adaptive outlier suppression layer that reduces sensitivity to isolated spikes while preserving detection of real regressions.

Summary of changes: Implemented a three-stage suppression strategy integrated into the existing canary scoring pipeline: 1) lightweight per-probe z-score normalization (robust to small sample sizes), 2) median-filtered spike-dampening that discounts single-sample deviations based on recent windowed variance, and 3) Bayesian weight recalibration that downweights probes with recurring discordant signals across slices. The suppression is parameterized and feature-flagged so operators can tune aggressiveness per-customer or per-route.

Why this is safe: The supp...

title: add-rolling-entropy-degradation-fence
preview: Summary: Introduces a Rolling Entropy Degradation Fence for canary rollouts. Motivation: percentile-based checks (p50/p95/p99) miss subtle distributional shifts where tails shift gradually or mass moves between latency modes. This PR implements an adaptive entropy-based detector that measures changes in the latency/...
text:
Summary: Introduces a Rolling Entropy Degradation Fence for canary rollouts. Motivation: percentile-based checks (p50/p95/p99) miss subtle distributional shifts where tails shift gradually or mass moves between latency modes. This PR implements an adaptive entropy-based detector that measures changes in the latency/throughput histogram distribution over a rolling window and fences rollouts when sustained distributional degradation is detected.

Design and behavior: The detector bins observed latency samples into an adaptive histogram (dynamic bucket sizing based on observed percentiles) and computes a rolling Shannon entropy plus a KL-divergence/JS-distance to a baseline histogram. We combine three signals: (1) instantaneous entropy change, (2) KL divergence to baseline, and (3) a sustained drift score computed with exponential decay. A canary fence is tripped only when the composite ...

title: implement signal smoothing and traffic-granularity guard for noisy tail regressions
preview: Motivation: Recent canary runs showed recurrent false-positive tail-latency alerts during short traffic spikes, and we observed noisy variance for small-batch inference workloads. This PR introduces a two-part defense: 1) a lightweight temporal signal smoother that reduces high-frequency noise in per-route latency f...
text:
Motivation: Recent canary runs showed recurrent false-positive tail-latency alerts during short traffic spikes, and we observed noisy variance for small-batch inference workloads. This PR introduces a two-part defense: 1) a lightweight temporal signal smoother that reduces high-frequency noise in per-route latency fingerprints while preserving changepoints, and 2) a traffic-granularity guard that slices canary evaluations by traffic orthogonals (route, payload-size bucket, and client tier) and gates rollouts when small, concentrated cohorts drive apparent regressions.

Goals: reduce alert churn, avoid unnecessary rollbacks for bursty micro-cohorts, and improve detection power for sustained regressions. This is intended as a conservative enhancement — default behavior is opt-in per-alert and the rollout guard has configurable thresholds and cooldown periods.

What changed (high-level):...

title: Add stratified noise calibrator and adaptive probe scheduler for low-signal canaries
preview: Context: Our canary detector has been producing noisy alerts for low-volume / heterogenous traffic slices (short queries, long queries, browser vs API) leading to unnecessary rollbacks. Motivation: reduce false positives and speed up meaningful detection in low-signal cohorts without increasing probe cost. This PR i...
text: Context: Our canary detector has been producing noisy alerts for low-volume / heterogenous traffic slices (short queries, long queries, browser vs API) leading to unnecessary rollbacks. Motivation: reduce false positives and speed up meaningful detection in low-signal cohorts without increasing probe cost. This PR introduces two complementary components: (1) Stratified Noise Calibrator (SNC) — a per-stratum robust noise estimator that derives baseline variance and Minimal Detectable Effect (MDE) using median-absolute-deviation + IQR trimming and an empirical noise model; and (2) Adaptive Probe Scheduler (APS) — a probe allocation layer that dynamically assigns probe budget to strata based on expected information gain (posterior variance reduction) and the user's rollout priority hints. Approach: SNC computes noise baselines per stratum (user_cohort, model_variant, sequence_length_buck...

title: Add latency-cliff detector and adaptive-concurrency rollout guard
preview: Motivation: We've observed hard 'latency cliff' failure modes where p95/p99 jump sharply within a short window even though rolling averages look stable; these events bypass percentile-based canaries and lead to partial outages. This PR implements a latency-cliff detector that detects rapid percentile deltas across s...
text: Motivation: We've observed hard 'latency cliff' failure modes where p95/p99 jump sharply within a short window even though rolling averages look stable; these events bypass percentile-based canaries and lead to partial outages. This PR implements a latency-cliff detector that detects rapid percentile deltas across short windows and wires an adaptive-concurrency rollout guard that temporarily reduces per-replica concurrency and pauses rollouts when a cliff is detected. Changes include detector algorithm, integration with the canary evaluation pipeline, a lightweight local load harness for reproducible cliffs, dashboards, and unit/integration tests. Checklist: [x] detector algorithm and unit tests, [x] canary integration and gating, [x] adaptive-concurrency policy and actuator, [x] dashboards and runbook, [x] CI flake fixes. Rollout plan: rollout as a guarded feature flag for 2 weeks, e...

title: Add stochastic concurrency fence and staggered probe scheduler to reduce probe-induced noise
preview: Context and motivation: We observed a recurring class of false-positive canary regressions when probe traffic arrives in short bursts or when client-side concurrency spikes align with canary probe windows. These cases amplify tail latency and create high variance in the canary signal, causing unnecessary rollbacks a...
text:
Context and motivation: We observed a recurring class of false-positive canary regressions when probe traffic arrives in short bursts or when client-side concurrency spikes align with canary probe windows. These cases amplify tail latency and create high variance in the canary signal, causing unnecessary rollbacks and developer churn. This PR implements two complementary mitigations: a stochastic concurrency fence (probabilistic admission control) to smooth bursty concurrent requests and a staggered probe scheduler to desynchronize probe timing across hosts.

What this changes (high level):
- Add perf-canary detector that computes `concurrency_entropy` per host and enables a probabilistic cap when entropy exceeds threshold.
- Replace existing probe dispatcher with a staggered scheduler that shards probe time windows to hosts and backfills missed probes without amplifying traffic.
- Ex...

title: Add billing meter regression scenarios for streaming, retries, and fallback routing
preview: Motivation: We’ve had multiple meter-related regressions where request-level behaviors (stream disconnects, server-side retry/fallback, and client retry with idempotency) caused double-counting or inconsistent attribution between (a) usage events and (b) the billing ledger. Unit/integration tests in the monorepo cov...
text:
Motivation: We’ve had multiple meter-related regressions where request-level behaviors (stream disconnects, server-side retry/fallback, and client retry with idempotency) caused double-counting or inconsistent attribution between (a) usage events and (b) the billing ledger. Unit/integration tests in the monorepo cover code paths, but we were missing an end-to-end canary that validates metering invariants against a live stack (gateway -> inference -> usage event emitter -> aggregation/export) under realistic failure modes.

This PR adds new perf-canary scenarios that intentionally trigger streaming + retry + fallback behaviors and then validate the expected metering invariants by querying the usage API / internal usage-events view within a bounded time window.

What’s included (new scenarios): 1) streaming_disconnect_no_double_count: Start a streaming chat completion, consume first N c...

title: Introduce sequential probe compositor and adaptive cutover allocator
preview: Motivation: improve canary probe efficiency and reduce probe-induced noise during rollouts by composing sequential probes and allocating cutover budgets adaptively based on recent telemetry. This change introduces a Sequential Probe Compositor that stitches short-window synthetic probes into prioritized sequences to...
text: Motivation: improve canary probe efficiency and reduce probe-induced noise during rollouts by composing sequential probes and allocating cutover budgets adaptively based on recent telemetry. This change introduces a Sequential Probe Compositor that stitches short-window synthetic probes into prioritized sequences to better surface phase-aligned regressions, and an Adaptive Cutover Allocator that computes per-rollout cutover budgets using traffic-conditioned stability scores and SLO-weighted credits. Key goals: lower false positives from probe collisions, preserve signal fidelity for tail latency, and enable safer gradual cutovers for large traffic shifts. Implementation notes: compositor merges probe windows across latency, throughput and error-rate signals; it maintains ordering and backpressure semantics so probes do not overload healthy replicas; allocator uses EWMA of percentile d...

title: Add rolling resilience-curve canary with tiered fallback and rate fallthrough
preview: Motivation: We observed slow, multi-hour regressions that did not trigger existing spike-focused detectors; this PR adds a time-weighted 'resilience curve' canary that measures cumulative degradation slope across cohorts and triggers tiered safeguards. High level: 1) rolling resilience curve evaluator that computes ...
text: Motivation: We observed slow, multi-hour regressions that did not trigger existing spike-focused detectors; this PR adds a time-weighted 'resilience curve' canary that measures cumulative degradation slope across cohorts and triggers tiered safeguards. High level: 1) rolling resilience curve evaluator that computes a decay-weighted degradation area under curve (AUC) for latency and error rate across multiple cohort slices (region, client-class, route). 2) temporal quorum voting: requires consensus from multiple independent cohorts before a hard fallback. 3) tiered fallback policy: soft fallback (traffic shaping + alert only), model-variant fallback (route to a lower-cost stable variant), and rate fallthrough (graceful request shedding with progressive SLAs). 4) adaptive probe scheduling that increases probe frequency for cohorts showing rising AUC and backs off when stabilized. Checkl...

title: Add TTFT/TPOT threshold profiles for FP8/INT8 kernel canaries (per-SKU baselines + noise suppression)
preview: Context: As part of the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint, we’re enabling quantized kernel modes behind flags and rolling out via perf canary. The existing canary gating uses a single threshold policy that was tuned on baseline (FP16/BF16) runs and has been noisy for quantized modes (especially when sample sizes a...
text: Context: As part of the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint, we’re enabling quantized kernel modes behind flags and rolling out via perf canary. The existing canary gating uses a single threshold policy that was tuned on baseline (FP16/BF16) runs and has been noisy for quantized modes (especially when sample sizes are small or when the scheduler mix shifts TTFT/TPOT differently). This PR introduces explicit threshold profiles for quantized kernels and per-SKU baselines so we can gate safely and reduce false positives while still catching real regressions. Summary of changes: (1) Add threshold profiles keyed by kernel_mode (baseline|fp8|int8) and sku (h100|a100|l4) with separate TTFT/TPOT constraints and minimum sample requirements. (2) Add noise suppression: a small-sample guardrail, robust aggregation using trimmed mean for p95 deltas, and an EWMA-based stability check that prevents ...

title: introduce adaptive sensitivity scheduler for canary detectors
preview: Motivation: long-running canary evaluators are sensitive to traffic volatility and changing baseline variance; fixed detection thresholds either generate noisy false positives during churn or miss subtle regressions when traffic is stable. This PR implements an Adaptive Sensitivity Scheduler (ASS) that continuously ...
text:
Motivation: long-running canary evaluators are sensitive to traffic volatility and changing baseline variance; fixed detection thresholds either generate noisy false positives during churn or miss subtle regressions when traffic is stable. This PR implements an Adaptive Sensitivity Scheduler (ASS) that continuously tunes detector sensitivity based on short-term traffic variance, cohort churn, and historical false-positive rate.

What it does (high level): introduces a scheduler that adjusts detector thresholds and aggregation windows at runtime. The scheduler observes three signals: instantaneous request-rate volatility, percentile drift across 1/5/15-minute windows, and the detector's empirical false-positive count. Using a lightweight Bayesian update, it adapts the detection sensitivity multiplier and the sample window size with safety bounds to avoid oscillation. Integrations added...

title: Add quantile-weighted, phase-aware throughput fence and severity scoring
preview: Problem: Existing canary detectors surface aggregate latency shifts but often miss multi-phase regressions where different traffic slices degrade at different times. We need a detector that (1) understands traffic phase boundaries (time-of-day, model warmup phases, routing slices) and (2) produces a single severity ...
text:
Problem: Existing canary detectors surface aggregate latency shifts but often miss multi-phase regressions where different traffic slices degrade at different times. We need a detector that (1) understands traffic phase boundaries (time-of-day, model warmup phases, routing slices) and (2) produces a single severity score that reflects both quantile skew and the persistence of the shift so rollout guards can make informed decisions.

This PR introduces a quantile-weighted, phase-aware throughput fence and a severity scoring function that folds into the canary evaluator pipeline. The detector computes quantile deltas per phase window and applies an exponential decay weighting across recent windows to emphasize sustained regressions while suppressing short-lived bursts. The severity score is a monotonic mapping of weighted-quantile deltas to [0,100] and is used by policy engines to trigg...

title: Add Quality Gate enforcement check stage to perf-canary pipeline
preview: Background: Optimize now requires quality gates before applying cost-saving config diffs (batching/cache/quantization). We’ve had a near-miss during beta where enforcement drifted between services (Optimize apply endpoint blocked correctly, rollout promotion didn’t). This PR adds a dedicated perf-canary stage that c...
text:
Background: Optimize now requires quality gates before applying cost-saving config diffs (batching/cache/quantization). We’ve had a near-miss during beta where enforcement drifted between services (Optimize apply endpoint blocked correctly, rollout promotion didn’t). This PR adds a dedicated perf-canary stage that continuously verifies gate enforcement semantics end-to-end against a controlled staging route, and alerts on regressions.

What this adds:
- New canary stage: `quality_gate_enforcement`
 - Executes an end-to-end scenario against staging Optimize + eval-harness + rollout-controller API surface.
 - Validates enforcement at the apply boundary (blocked when gate != PASSED) and at promotion boundary (canary promotion blocked on failed gate).
- A small, deterministic test fixture:
 - Uses a dedicated canary account + route (non-production) and a synthetic config diff that is expe...

title: Add representative workload suite to nightly canary + gating mode for release branches
preview: Context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” project, we need the representative suite to run continuously (nightly) and also to be usable as a release gate for runtime release branches. This PR wires the representative suite runner into perf-canary with two execution modes: (1) ni...
text: Context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” project, we need the representative suite to run continuously (nightly) and also to be usable as a release gate for runtime release branches. This PR wires the representative suite runner into perf-canary with two execution modes: (1) nightly mode (signal + trend) and (2) gating mode (pass/fail) intended for release/* branches. Summary of changes: - Adds a new job definition for the representative suite (chat streaming, long-context prefill, embeddings high-QPS; tool-calling is stubbed in config but not enabled by default yet). - Introduces a `mode` flag: `nightly` vs `gating`. Nightly publishes full metric payloads and trends; gating evaluates a smaller set of canonical metrics against threshold config and emits a single PASS/FAIL with links to artifacts. - Adds threshold configuration files keyed by (mod...

title: Shadow traffic overhead benchmark suite (routing mirror + telemetry) for go/no-go gates
preview: Context: As part of Shadow Traffic (quality eval metrics) we need a repeatable way to quantify overhead from (a) request mirroring to shadow targets, (b) additional telemetry emission, and (c) backpressure/circuit breaker behavior under scoring/ingestion degradation. This suite is intended to run as a go/no-go gate ...
text:
Context: As part of Shadow Traffic (quality eval metrics) we need a repeatable way to quantify overhead from (a) request mirroring to shadow targets, (b) additional telemetry emission, and (c) backpressure/circuit breaker behavior under scoring/ingestion degradation. This suite is intended to run as a go/no-go gate for beta/GA (and as a nightly regression check).

What this PR does: 1) Adds a benchmark harness to perf-canary that drives realistic inference traffic (chat + streaming chat) through the Redwood gateway with shadowing enabled/disabled and varying sampling rates. 2) Captures user-path latency deltas and resource overhead while ensuring fail-open semantics (shadow path failures must not move user-path SLOs). 3) Emits a standardized results bundle (JSON + markdown) for consumption in rollout readiness docs and for CI gating.

Scenarios included: - Chat (non-streaming): 256 an...

title: Add baseline fetch + regression evaluator (min samples, rolling window, pct thresholds)
preview: Implements the baseline lookup path and the first pass of regression evaluation logic for perf-canary. This is the missing piece between “we can generate load + emit raw latency/tps metrics” and “we can decide whether something is actually regressing (and do so with low noise)”. The evaluator is intentionally conser...
text:
Implements the baseline lookup path and the first pass of regression evaluation logic for perf-canary. This is the missing piece between “we can generate load + emit raw latency/tps metrics” and “we can decide whether something is actually regressing (and do so with low noise)”. The evaluator is intentionally conservative for phase 1: it enforces minimum sample sizes, uses a rolling window over recent canary runs, and compares observed vs baseline using percent-change thresholds per metric.

Context / why: We’ve seen early canary runs spike due to batch composition variance and telemetry ingestion jitter. We need a consistent evaluation step that (a) won’t flap on small sample sizes, (b) is explicit about confidence, and (c) produces a stable verdict we can wire into alert rules + rollout gates.

What’s included:
- Baseline client: fetches baseline artifacts from the Baseline Store se...

title: Add mixed-length burst scenario + thresholds for KV compaction regressions
preview: Motivation: The KV cache compaction work (runtime-1.22) needs a perf-canary signal that reliably reproduces fragmentation + churn patterns we see in production, and that fails fast when compaction/eviction introduces decode stalls or tail latency spikes. Existing canary scenarios (steady decode, long prompts) don’t ...
text:
Motivation: The KV cache compaction work (runtime-1.22) needs a perf-canary signal that reliably reproduces fragmentation + churn patterns we see in production, and that fails fast when compaction/eviction introduces decode stalls or tail latency spikes. Existing canary scenarios (steady decode, long prompts) don’t create enough allocator churn to surface the regressions we saw in staging.

This PR adds a new scenario (`mixed_length_burst`) that generates mixed prompt lengths + bursty arrivals + cancellations, and wires pass/fail thresholds + alert routing that are specific to compaction regressions.

What’s included: - New canary scenario: mixed-length burst (configurable length distribution + burst profile) - KPI collection updates: capture p95/p99 latency, TTFT, throughput, and the runtime-exposed compaction/eviction signals when present (bytes moved / compaction time / eviction ra...

title: perf-canary: add QPS rate limits + per-region capacity budget (GPU overhead guardrail)
preview: Context: As we move perf-canary from staging into a production region, we need hard guardrails so the canary cannot consume meaningful GPU capacity or accidentally fan out due to config mistakes. This PR implements (1) explicit QPS caps with a token-bucket limiter, and (2) a per-region capacity budget that converts ...
text:
Context: As we move perf-canary from staging into a production region, we need hard guardrails so the canary cannot consume meaningful GPU capacity or accidentally fan out due to config mistakes. This PR implements (1) explicit QPS caps with a token-bucket limiter, and (2) a per-region capacity budget that converts an overhead target (e.g., 0.5% of region GPU capacity) into an allowed request rate/concurrency for the canary. The goal is: safe by default, predictable overhead, and an immediate kill-switch when budgets are exceeded.

What this adds (high-level): - Rate limiting: token bucket limiter at the request scheduler layer (covers both steady-state QPS and burst). - Capacity budget: `capacity_budget` config per region (defaults to 0.003 = 0.3% until we tune) with optional overrides per tier/model group. - Budget accounting: estimates GPU-seconds/request using (a) last known basel...

title: Add synthetic canary for graceful degradation path (model/region fallback)
preview: Summary: This adds a new synthetic canary test that exercises Redwood’s graceful degradation behavior (fallback model and/or fallback region) and emits low-cardinality signals that map directly to incident taxonomy categories (Routing/Fallback misbehavior, Capacity/Fleet shortage, Control plane/routing config). Moti...
text: Summary: This adds a new synthetic canary test that exercises Redwood’s graceful degradation behavior (fallback model and/or fallback region) and emits low-cardinality signals that map directly to incident taxonomy categories (Routing/Fallback misbehavior, Capacity/Fleet shortage, Control plane/routing config). Motivation: Recent incidents and taxonomy workshops highlighted that we don’t currently have a reliable, continuous signal that the “degrade gracefully” path is actually working end-to-end (router decision -> fallback selection -> request succeeds -> correct fallback_reason emitted). This canary is intentionally not a throughput/perf benchmark; it is a correctness + reliability smoke test with a small number of requests on a fixed cadence. What it does: (1) Sends a small chat completion request using a policy that prefers a primary model/region, but is allowed to fall back to a...

title: traffic burst shielding and graceful-degradation rollout guard
preview: Motivation: we see a class of incidents where sudden client-driven traffic bursts (spikes in request ingress slope) overwhelm KV-cache memory and batching heuristics, producing high tail latency and queueing even when nominal throughput is within capacity. Existing canary detectors focus on gradual regressions or st...
text:
Motivation: we see a class of incidents where sudden client-driven traffic bursts (spikes in request ingress slope) overwhelm KV-cache memory and batching heuristics, producing high tail latency and queueing even when nominal throughput is within capacity. Existing canary detectors focus on gradual regressions or static threshold crossings; they tend to either miss very short sharp bursts or trigger hard rollbacks that are disruptive for live rollouts.

This PR introduces 'Burst Shielding' — a new detector + graded mitigation rollout-guard that: 1) detects sudden traffic bursts using a fused signal of p99 latency delta, request ingress slope, request queue depth, and per-device KV cache eviction rate; 2) classifies burst severity with a Nyquist-inspired adaptive smoothing and causal-change detector to avoid false positives on regular traffic ramps; 3) applies graded, time-bound mitiga...

title: Add region + hardware SKU dimension config (with fanout safeguards)
preview: ## Summary This PR adds first-class configuration for running perf-canary across multiple **regions** and **hardware SKUs**, while ensuring we don’t accidentally fan out to every region/SKU combination (and burn the overhead budget). The canary runner now supports: (1) Target selection by `region` and `hardware_sku`...
text:
## Summary This PR adds first-class configuration for running perf-canary across multiple **regions** and **hardware SKUs**, while ensuring we don’t accidentally fan out to every region/SKU combination (and burn the overhead budget). The canary runner now supports: (1) Target selection by `region` and `hardware_sku` (optional), (2) A hard fanout guard that defaults to conservative behavior, and (3) Safer defaults for Helm/Env-based deployments. This is needed for Phase 1 rollout where we want to start in `us-east` on a single SKU, then expand gradually to `eu-west` and additional SKUs once baselines/thresholds are tuned.

## Motivation / Context Today the canary targets are effectively “global” (implicitly whatever the control plane routes to). That makes it hard to: - Attribute regressions to fleet changes (SKU-specific) vs runtime changes - Set region-specific baselines (telemetry v...

title: Add log redaction + no-PII assertions for perf-canary request/telemetry path
preview: Motivation: perf-canary must never emit or persist customer data. While the canary uses synthetic prompts, we still had a few sharp edges where (a) debug logging could print request bodies on errors and (b) some error/reporting paths would include raw prompt text / user-provided identifiers if they ever slipped thro...
text: Motivation: perf-canary must never emit or persist customer data. While the canary uses synthetic prompts, we still had a few sharp edges where (a) debug logging could print request bodies on errors and (b) some error/reporting paths would include raw prompt text / user-provided identifiers if they ever slipped through config. This PR adds explicit redaction + hard assertions so we fail-closed and keep logs/metrics safe. Summary of changes: (1) Introduce a centralized redaction helper for log fields and error payloads; (2) Add runtime assertions that enforce "synthetic-only" prompt sources (no external prompt injection), with an opt-out only for local dev; (3) Ensure we never log request bodies, prompt strings, or headers that may contain identifiers; (4) Add "safe fields" allowlist for alert webhook payloads so incident-bot never receives prompt content; (5) Add tests that simulate e...

title: perf-canary: add standardized alert webhook payload format for incident-bot
preview: Context: incident-bot ingestion currently relies on parsing semi-structured strings emitted by perf-canary. This has made dedupe noisy and makes it hard to attach the right dashboard/runbook links (esp. when we start running canary per region + SKU). This PR introduces a versioned, stable webhook payload schema (v1)...
text:
Context: incident-bot ingestion currently relies on parsing semi-structured strings emitted by perf-canary. This has made dedupe noisy and makes it hard to attach the right dashboard/runbook links (esp. when we start running canary per region + SKU). This PR introduces a versioned, stable webhook payload schema (v1) and a small formatter that produces consistent alert payloads across warning/page severities.

What this changes: Adds a `WebhookPayloadV1` struct + builder with a single entrypoint `FormatAlertPayload(ctx, evalResult, linkSet)`. The payload includes the primary identifiers (model, model_version, region, tier, hardware_sku), regression metrics (observed vs baseline, deltas, thresholds), confidence/sample sizing details, and a normalized set of links (Grafana dashboard, logs query, trace search, runbook). The incident-bot handler will use these fields for dedupe and renderi...

title: Add synthetic prompt generator + prompt-shape buckets (seedable workload profiles)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a synthetic prompt-shape generator and a seedable workload profile so perf-canary runs are reproducible across regions/hosts and can be tuned without touching code. The goal is to approximate production request shapes (prefill/decode mix, prompt length buckets) without using any customer prompt...
text: Summary: This PR adds a synthetic prompt-shape generator and a seedable workload profile so perf-canary runs are reproducible across regions/hosts and can be tuned without touching code. The goal is to approximate production request shapes (prefill/decode mix, prompt length buckets) without using any customer prompts or identifiers. Why: Baselines and regression detection require stable distributions. Early canary scaffolding used a fixed prompt and random lengths, which made p95 latency and tokens/sec variance too high and made comparisons across runtime versions noisy. What’s included: 1) PromptShape + WorkloadProfile config types (YAML) with explicit length buckets and weights; 2) Seeded RNG for selecting shapes, generation params, and per-run determinism; 3) A small library that produces “prompt skeletons” (shape only) that can be filled by a static, non-sensitive text corpus ship...
links: Next PR: pr-19212-emit-metrics-latency-tokens-per-sec.json (wires shapes into metric breakdowns)

title: Emit metrics for end-to-end latency and tokens/sec (per-model) with cardinality guardrails
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-pass metric emission for perf-canary runs: end-to-end latency histograms and tokens/sec (plus supporting counters) with per-model attribution. Metrics are emitted from the canary process (not the runtime) so we can alert even when runtime telemetry pipelines are delayed. Motivation: We ne...
text: Summary: This PR adds first-pass metric emission for perf-canary runs: end-to-end latency histograms and tokens/sec (plus supporting counters) with per-model attribution. Metrics are emitted from the canary process (not the runtime) so we can alert even when runtime telemetry pipelines are delayed. Motivation: We need low-noise, per-model signals for p50/p95/p99 latency and throughput (tokens/sec) before we can wire dashboards/alerting. This also sets the label policy up front to avoid a repeat of past cardinality blowups (e.g., per-prompt identifiers or unconstrained model strings). Metrics added (Prometheus/OpenTelemetry bridge): perf_canary_request_e2e_latency_ms (histogram; attributes: model_id, model_version, region, tier, hardware_sku, outcome); perf_canary_request_tokens_per_second (histogram; computed from generated_tokens / decode_time; same attributes); perf_canary_generated...

title: perf-canary: add optimization config safeguards + gate signal publisher for latency regressions
preview:
Title: perf-canary: add optimization config safeguards + gate signal publisher for latency regressions

Context
Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs needs an external, repeatable “canary judge” that can detect config-induced latency regressions early and produce machine-readable gate signals. Today, on-cal...
text:
Title: perf-canary: add optimization config safeguards + gate signal publisher for latency regressions

Context
Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs needs an external, repeatable “canary judge” that can detect config-induced latency regressions early and produce machine-readable gate signals. Today, on-call relies on dashboards + manual judgement, which slows progression and increases risk (see internal incident notes around p95 spikes after caching/batching tweaks).

What this PR does
- Adds a new scheduled/on-demand canary workflow ("optimization-safeguard") that:
 - Detects active Optimization Config rollout scopes (tenant/model/region + canary %) and config_version.
 - Pulls baseline vs canary metrics (p95/p99 latency + 5xx rate) for the last N minutes, with warmup handling.
 - Evaluates against configurable thresholds with sane defaults (latency regression % + absolute ...

title: Bootstrap perf-canary service skeleton (config + request loop)
preview: Summary: This PR bootstraps the initial perf-canary service skeleton: repo layout, config parsing/validation, and a basic request loop that can hit internal inference endpoints on a schedule. This is intentionally minimal (no metrics emission yet, no baseline evaluation yet) so we can land the scaffolding early and ...
text:
Summary: This PR bootstraps the initial perf-canary service skeleton: repo layout, config parsing/validation, and a basic request loop that can hit internal inference endpoints on a schedule. This is intentionally minimal (no metrics emission yet, no baseline evaluation yet) so we can land the scaffolding early and iterate behind feature flags.

Context: We need a lightweight, always-on canary workload in prod to catch serving-runtime regressions (p95 latency + tokens/sec) before broad customer impact. This PR is the first building block: a small service that can execute synthetic requests against a curated model list and report per-run status via logs. Follow-ups will add: (1) synthetic prompt generation (shape-only; no customer prompts), (2) structured metrics + histograms, (3) baseline fetch + regression evaluation, and (4) rate limiting/capacity budgeting.

What’s in this PR:
- Ne...

title: Add perf-canary gates for hardware profile rollouts (latency + throughput) and adjust alert routing
preview: Context: As part of the hardware tuning profiles pack rollout (H100/L40S first, B200 gated), we need perf-canary to catch regressions that only show up when `hardware_profile_pack` is enabled and the runtime selects SKU-specific presets (scheduler/kernels/KV-cache budgets). Existing canary jobs compare runtime build...
text:
Context: As part of the hardware tuning profiles pack rollout (H100/L40S first, B200 gated), we need perf-canary to catch regressions that only show up when `hardware_profile_pack` is enabled and the runtime selects SKU-specific presets (scheduler/kernels/KV-cache budgets). Existing canary jobs compare runtime builds against a baseline, but they don’t segment by “profile-enabled” vs “profile-disabled”, and alerts are currently too noisy (especially on L40S runners per INT-8832). This PR adds explicit gates for profile-enabled builds, tightens/loosens thresholds where appropriate, and updates alert routing so actionable regressions page the right rotation.

What’s in this PR: (1) New gate group `hardware_profiles_rollout` that runs paired experiments (A/B) with `RUNTIME_ENABLE_HARDWARE_PROFILE_PACK=1` (treatment) vs `=0` (control) for the same build SHA where possible, and evaluates de...

title: add-load-diffusion-prober-and-smooth-fallback-oracle
preview: Motivation: production rollouts occasionally experienced sharp, short-duration latency spikes when traffic shifts concentrated on a small set of worker shards. This PR introduces a traffic-aware Load Diffusion Prober and a Smooth Fallback Oracle to reduce false-positive rollbacks and provide graceful stall protectio...
text: Motivation: production rollouts occasionally experienced sharp, short-duration latency spikes when traffic shifts concentrated on a small set of worker shards. This PR introduces a traffic-aware Load Diffusion Prober and a Smooth Fallback Oracle to reduce false-positive rollbacks and provide graceful stall protection during bursty traffic. The Load Diffusion Prober continuously samples routing keys and estimates effective shard utilization and collision probability; when hotspots are detected it adjusts probe weights and issues soft-penalties instead of hard rollbacks. The Smooth Fallback Oracle consumes prober signals plus percentile-residual smoothing to decide fallback windows and maximum rollback budgets. Implementation: - lightweight prober module with opt-in sampling policy tied to rollout gates - reservoir-based diffusion estimator that adapts window size by observed burstiness...

title: Add canary scenarios for predictive autoscaling (traffic ramps + periodic spikes)
preview:
## Summary
Adds two new perf-canary workloads intended to catch latency regressions and oscillation when Dedicated predictive autoscaling is enabled:
1) `predictive_ramp_30m`: steady baseline then a monotonic ramp (to mimic “daily ramp” customers).
2) `predictive_spikes_10m`: periodic spikes with short recovery wind...
text:
## Summary
Adds two new perf-canary workloads intended to catch latency regressions and oscillation when Dedicated predictive autoscaling is enabled:
1) `predictive_ramp_30m`: steady baseline then a monotonic ramp (to mimic “daily ramp” customers).
2) `predictive_spikes_10m`: periodic spikes with short recovery windows (to mimic bursty SaaS/cron-driven traffic).

These scenarios run against a Dedicated deployment with predictive mode toggled via canary config (feature-flagged per account) and record:
- p50/p95/p99 end-to-end latency
- queue depth (when available)
- autoscaler desired vs bounded desired replicas (to detect max-bound saturation and clamp behavior)
- scale event reasons + step sizes (to detect oscillation)
- prediction freshness + error (if forecast provider is enabled in the canary env)

The goal is to gate pilot expansions: predictive mode should not regress p95 during...

title: Rollout canary safety smoke tests (cohorting + auto-stop wiring) for staging
preview: Summary: This PR adds a small suite of staging-only safety smoke tests for Hosted canary rollouts. The goal is to catch two classes of beta issues before they reach customers: (1) cohorting / split enforcement regressions (e.g., observed split mismatch per region) and (2) stop-condition wiring regressions (e.g., SLO...
text:
Summary: This PR adds a small suite of staging-only safety smoke tests for Hosted canary rollouts. The goal is to catch two classes of beta issues before they reach customers: (1) cohorting / split enforcement regressions (e.g., observed split mismatch per region) and (2) stop-condition wiring regressions (e.g., SLO breach evaluates but orchestrator doesn’t pause/rollback, or rollout step stalls on metric timeouts). These tests are intentionally narrow, deterministic, and cleanup-safe so they can be run as part of GA change management.

Motivation / context: During beta we saw an incident-like split mismatch in us-east (SUP-48377) and a separate class of failures where metric evaluation timeouts could stall progress (see follow-ups in ENG-9158 / pr-28895). We already have E2E coverage in the monorepo (ENG-9164), but we wanted a lightweight “canary of the canary” that runs against stag...

title: Add predictive concurrency allocator with SLO-gating for canary routing
preview: Motivation: Canary evaluations often surface regressions caused by unexpected concurrency pressure on GPUs and model KV caches. This PR introduces a predictive concurrency allocator that uses short-window time-series features and a lightweight online estimator to predict occupancy and limit per-model concurrency bef...
text: Motivation: Canary evaluations often surface regressions caused by unexpected concurrency pressure on GPUs and model KV caches. This PR introduces a predictive concurrency allocator that uses short-window time-series features and a lightweight online estimator to predict occupancy and limit per-model concurrency before tail latency exceeds SLO thresholds. It ties into the existing canary pipeline so canaries can gate rollouts automatically when predicted saturation is likely. Design summary: - Runtime component: a new allocator service that subscribes to per-model throughput/latency streams and predicts short-term concurrency headroom using exponential smoothing and a small linear regressor. The allocator returns a recommended concurrency cap and a confidence score. - Canary integration: canary scheduler consults the allocator before opening traffic; if predicted tail-latency exceeds ...

title: Add canary for telemetry query latency (incident-bot diagnostics guardrail)
preview:
## Context
Incident-bot v2 relies on the telemetry query service to render saturation/context cards in the first ~60–90s of an incident thread. We’ve seen query latency spikes during incidents (bursty usage + upstream timeouts) that cause empty/partial cards and slow responder workflows. This PR adds a dedicated per...
text:
## Context
Incident-bot v2 relies on the telemetry query service to render saturation/context cards in the first ~60–90s of an incident thread. We’ve seen query latency spikes during incidents (bursty usage + upstream timeouts) that cause empty/partial cards and slow responder workflows. This PR adds a dedicated perf canary to continuously validate telemetry query latency and error behavior under a representative diagnostic workload.

Related:
- INT-3341 (telemetry query timeouts from incident-bot)
- ENG-5427 (telemetry query cache/coalescing)
- ENG-5429 (bot degraded-mode + load shedding)

## What this adds
1) New canary: `telemetry-query-latency`
- Runs every 60s (configurable) from 3 regions: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1
- Executes a fixed set of low-cardinality queries that mirror incident-bot cards:
 - GPU utilization (pool-level, region scoped)
 - Queue depth / scheduler back...

title: Add quantization profile canary scenarios (baseline vs profile-enabled) with thresholds + bisect hints
preview: Summary: This PR adds perf-canary coverage for the Quantization Profile Library route-level enablement by introducing paired scenarios that run baseline (default precision) vs profile-enabled execution for the same model/workload mix, then compares deltas against explicit thresholds. The goal is to catch (a) tail-la...
text: Summary: This PR adds perf-canary coverage for the Quantization Profile Library route-level enablement by introducing paired scenarios that run baseline (default precision) vs profile-enabled execution for the same model/workload mix, then compares deltas against explicit thresholds. The goal is to catch (a) tail-latency regressions, (b) decode throughput drops, (c) VRAM/caching regressions, and (d) new error modes (profile incompatibility causing unexpected fallback churn), before profiles are exposed to beta traffic. Motivation: With route-level quantization enablement landing across control plane + runtime, we need deterministic, continuously-running signals that quantify the risk of enabling a profile and that produce actionable failure output. These scenarios are intended to run on the same infra as existing decode/tps canaries and to surface “bisect hints” that point engineers t...

title: Add cache telemetry overhead perf-canary gate (p95 regression threshold)
preview: Adds a new perf-canary workload and CI gate to quantify runtime overhead from the prefix/KV cache telemetry instrumentation. The canary runs a stable request mix with caching enabled and toggles telemetry flags to compare p95 end-to-end latency (prefill+decode) against a baseline. If the measured p95 regression exce...
text:
Adds a new perf-canary workload and CI gate to quantify runtime overhead from the prefix/KV cache telemetry instrumentation. The canary runs a stable request mix with caching enabled and toggles telemetry flags to compare p95 end-to-end latency (prefill+decode) against a baseline. If the measured p95 regression exceeds the configured threshold, the canary fails to prevent rollouts that silently add latency overhead.

Motivation: We’re rolling out new cache metrics (hit/miss counters, eviction pressure, wasted prefill) behind flags and with sampling, but we need a hard stop in CI to keep instrumentation overhead near-zero (especially at p95/p99). This PR is intended to be the guardrail referenced by the runtime rollout plan and SRE approval thread.

What’s included: (1) New scenario `cache_telemetry_overhead` that replays a prompt set designed to exercise prefill, decode, prefix reuse,...

title: Add canary check for output-token metering parity (streaming + non-streaming)
preview: Context: we’ve had at least one production regression where streaming responses under-counted output tokens in metering, which then cascaded into Unit Economics dashboards (blended $/1M output tokens) and reconciliation views. This PR adds an automated canary that detects output-token metering regressions before a g...
text:
Context: we’ve had at least one production regression where streaming responses under-counted output tokens in metering, which then cascaded into Unit Economics dashboards (blended $/1M output tokens) and reconciliation views. This PR adds an automated canary that detects output-token metering regressions before a gateway/runtime change is widely rolled out.

What this adds:
- New canary suite `metering_output_token_parity` that runs against a small allowlisted canary tenant in prod.
- Exercises BOTH streaming and non-streaming generation paths (including tool-call shaped outputs) and validates:
 1) output_tokens reported by the API response metadata
 2) output_tokens recorded by metering events (usage pipeline)
 3) that the delta between (1) and (2) is within a configured tolerance after accounting for ingestion latency
- Adds guardrails for ingestion lag: the canary polls the meteri...

title: add-frequency-domain-residuals-detector-and-graceful-threshold-adapter
preview: Motivation: transient, periodic interference (GC sweeps, noisy neighbors, periodic telemetry jobs) has caused low-amplitude latency regressions that percentile-only detectors occasionally miss and that can trigger noisy rollbacks. This PR introduces a new detector that analyzes latency time-series in the frequency d...
text: Motivation: transient, periodic interference (GC sweeps, noisy neighbors, periodic telemetry jobs) has caused low-amplitude latency regressions that percentile-only detectors occasionally miss and that can trigger noisy rollbacks. This PR introduces a new detector that analyzes latency time-series in the frequency domain and fuses spectral residual signals with existing percentile drift metrics to produce a more stable canary signal. Design summary: 1) Windowed FFT transform of per-request p95/p99 time series over short sliding windows (configurable); 2) Compute spectral residual energy in targeted bands (e.g., 0.5-5s cycles) and normalize by traffic power to be traffic-aware; 3) Produce a residuals_score time series and combine with percentile delta via a probabilistic fusion step (logistic weight based on signal SNR); 4) Graceful Threshold Adapter: thresholds are scaled by a lightwe...
